pages.text:
Yo, I really suck at starting these but I'll get straight to the point.

I'm sorry.

Idk how genuine it might sound here, but I genuinely apologize for my past actions.

I won't yap about all that I'm sorry about but it's to generalize it's everything that has bothered you which I turned a blind eye to.

Also I don't know when I'll share this message with you, but I know it will be when I feel it's right.

I've changed.

I hope what I've improved on shows in my words and actions, even if not I just hope nothing I said or did - in the however long it has been since I broke no contact - has made you feel uncomfortable. 

I didn't say sorry on the day I broke no contact cuz I didn't want it to feel forced, or rather a sorry out of desperation. However, today I say sorry because I've changed my actions for what I apologize about.

I haven't really made it a priority to deduce or investigate about what you think about me now or what opinions you have, because then I'd fall into a rabbit hole of assumptions - which you never liked anyways - so can I ask just one question in this entire message. How do you feel about me now? What are your opinions? What could I improve or what do you still not like?

I guess I lied about the one just one question, but I'm eager to know your answer.

Also I'm writing this on a random day, so nothing I say here is at all influenced by how we've been talking (if we still are) recently or any thing that has happened between us. I have no date decided so you might be seeing this a day, a month or even a year from now.

Ok I lied, looks like I did yap.

I realized one thing in the 8 months we haven't talked, everything was avoidable if I just listened to your criticism you stable in front of my face. I'm referring to the pictures below, when I should have said sorry and changed myself, I decided to defend myself and play the victim.
